science.title: Scientists catch antimatter “atom” acting like a wave for the first time
science.summary: Quantum physics once shocked scientists by revealing that particles can behave like waves—and now, that strange behavior has been pushed even further. science.title: Light can now be shaped in empty space, and it could simplify sensing and boost data links
science.summary: Scientists at the University of East Anglia have uncovered a hidden property of light that allows it to twist, spin and behave differently—without mirrors, materials or special lenses. science.title: Urban birds fear women more than men, and scientists don't know why
science.summary: An international team of researchers have made the surprising discovery that urban birds—such as great tits, house sparrows and blackbirds—flee sooner when approached by women compared to men. But they don't understand why. Conducted across five European countries, the study involved male and female participants (match

science.title: Breakthrough Crystal Lets Scientists “Write” Nanoscale Patterns With Light
science.summary: A team of scientists has uncovered a crystal that can be reshaped and programmed using ordinary light, opening a new path for building optical technology. Researchers at the XPANCEO Emerging Technologies Research Center, working alongside Nobel Laureate Prof. Konstantin Novoselov (University of Manchester and the Natio

science.title: Scientists just captured a mysterious quantum “dance” inside superconductors
science.summary: In a breakthrough experiment, scientists directly imaged how particles pair up in a system that mimics superconductors. Instead of behaving independently, the pairs moved in a synchronized, dance-like pattern—something never predicted before. This suggests a major gap in the classic theory of superconductivity.

tech.title: Humanoid robots start sorting luggage in Tokyo airport test amid labor shortage
tech.summary: Humanoid robots could load cargo and clean aircraft cabins at Haneda Airport.

tech.title: Start with the sensors, then design the rest: How Zoox built its robotaxi
tech.summary: The bidirectional design has some clear advantages for a working taxi.

tech.title: Taylor Swift is stepping up the legal war on AI copycats
tech.summary: Taylor Swift has been at the center of AI imitation controversies for years, and now, she's become the latest celebrity who's escalating attempts to protect herself from AI copycats. tech.title: Get Ready for More Brain-Scanning Consumer Gadgets
tech.summary: Neurable, which makes noninvasive brain-computer interfaces, is licensing its technology and promises a “flood” of new third-party hardware this year and next.

medicine.title: From gut to brain: Scientists engineer bacteria to treat severe liver-related brain dysfunction
medicine.summary: When the liver fails, toxins—such as ammonia—that should be filtered from the blood build up and reach the brain. The result is hepatic encephalopathy (HE), a devastating neurological complication of liver disease that can cause anxiety, confusion, memory loss and, in severe cases, coma. medicine.title: Simple “gut reset” may stop weight gain after Ozempic or Wegovy
medicine.summary: A new minimally invasive procedure may help people keep weight off after stopping popular drugs like Ozempic and semaglutide—something most patients struggle with. In a clinical trial, those who underwent a technique called duodenal mucosal resurfacing regained far less weight compared to others after discontinuing the

medicine.title: Hidden virus in your gut may be linked to colon cancer
medicine.summary: A newly discovered virus hiding inside a common gut bacterium could help explain one of medicine’s long-standing mysteries: why a microbe found in both healthy people and cancer patients is linked to colorectal cancer. The research suggests that the interaction between bacteria and the viruses they carry may be key to

medicine.title: Two common drugs may reverse fatty liver disease, study finds
medicine.summary: Scientists have discovered that combining two existing drugs can dramatically reduce liver fat linked to a common and often silent disease. The treatment not only improved liver health in animal models but also showed potential to lower heart-related risks. Interestingly, using lower doses of both drugs together worked

medicine.title: This cheap drug could help people with type 1 diabetes use less insulin
medicine.summary: A surprising new clinical trial has revealed that metformin—a cheap, century-old drug widely used for type 2 diabetes—may help people with type 1 diabetes in an unexpected way. mathematics.title: In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far?
mathematics.summary: The quest to make mathematics rigorous has a long and spotty history — one mathematicians can learn from as they push to formalize everything in the computer program Lean. mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: The Math That Explains Why Bell Curves Are Everywhere

space.summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launched 25 Starlink internet satellites to orbit from California on Sunday (April 26). It was the company's 50th liftoff of the year already.

astronomy.title: Could Light Alone Get Us to Another Star?
astronomy.summary: Using nothing but a laser beam, scientists at Texas A&M University have demonstrated that tiny engineered devices can be lifted and steered in three dimensions without any physical contact. astronomy.title: The Planet Haul That Changes Everything.
astronomy.summary: NASA's planet hunting telescope has been busy. A new study has just sifted through the light of over 83 million stars and emerged with more than 11,000 potential worlds, including a confirmed giant planet orbiting a distant star. The results don't just add to our catalogue of planets. They fundamentally change where we

astronomy.title: JWST Hunts for an 'Earth-Moon' Twin in a Habitable Zone, But the Star Has Other Plans
astronomy.summary: The Moon has played a huge role in the development of Earth. It stabilizes the planet, tempered dramatic climate swings, and possibly even provided the tidal heating that might have led to the first life forms. astronomy.title: Catching the Earliest Stars in the Universe
astronomy.summary: New observations from the James Webb Space Telescope might have caught the signature of the universe's first stars. The post Catching the Earliest Stars in the Universe appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Astronomers Find the Edge of the Milky Way
astronomy.summary: Astronomers have located the edge of the Milky Way’s star-forming disk for the first time, showing that star formation is focused within 40,000 light-years of our galactic center. The post Astronomers Find the Edge of the Milky Way appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

architecture.summary: Architecture studios Diamond Schmitt, MVRDV and Two Row Architect have revealed the design for the Temerty Building at the University of Toronto, a research building informed by Indigenous design principles. ai.title: OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents come to AWS
